units.unit_name: Thermal Energy and Changes of Matter

questions.ex_title: Write the scientific term of each of the following statements
questions.q_txt: Ignition of materials without the presence of an external source of heat
questions.a_txt: Spontaneous combustion.

questions.ex_title: Write the scientific term of each of the following statements
questions.q_txt: A chemical process in which the percentage of oxygen increases in the substance or the percentage of hydrogen decreases in it.
questions.a_txt: Oxidation process.

questions.ex_title: Write the scientific term of each of the following statements
questions.q_txt: A chemical process in which the percentage of oxygen decreases in the substance or the percentage of hydrogen increases in it.
questions.a_txt: Reduction process.

questions.ex_title: Write the scientific term of each of the following statements
questions.q_txt: The substance that takes away oxygen or gives hydrogen during the chemical reaction.
questions.a_txt: Reducing agent.

questions.ex_title: Write the scientific term of each of the following statements
questions.q_txt: The substance that gives oxygen or takes away hydrogen during the chemical reaction.
questions.a_txt: Oxidizing agent.

questions.q_txt: The reaction of glycerin with potassium permanganate powder is _____ reaction and produces a flame of _____ colour.
In atmospheric air, _____ gas neither burns nor supports combustion, while _____ gas supports combustion.
_____ principle is used for extinguishing sodium fires, while _____ principle is used for extinguishing fire resulting from natural gas leakage.
When hydrogen gas is passed over hot copper oxide, copper oxide turns into _____ whose colour is _____.
In the reaction: H2 + CuO -> Cu + H2O Hydrogen gas undergoes _____ process, and copper oxide undergoes _____ process.
The oxidizing agent undergoes _____ process, while the reducing agent undergoes _____ process.
In the reaction: 2Mg + CO2 -> 2MgO + C Magnesium undergoes _____ process, so it is the _____ agent.
Water is used in extinguishing magnesium fires.
questions.a_txt: Sand.

questions.q_txt: Hydrogen gas is used in fire extinguishers to isolate fires from air.
questions.a_txt: Carbon dioxide.

questions.q_txt: In the reaction: H2 + CuO -> Cu + H2O, hydrogen acts as the oxidizing agent.
questions.a_txt: Copper oxide.

questions.ex_title: Mark (T) or (X) for each statement, with correction if needed
questions.q_txt: Hay burns in some villages by non-spontaneous combustion due to the activity of bacterial decomposition.

questions.ex_title: Mark (T) or (X) for each statement, with correction if needed
questions.q_txt: Gas valves are closed during fires to isolate oxygen.

questions.ex_title: Mark (T) or (X) for each statement, with correction if needed
questions.q_txt: Water is characterized by its low specific heat.

questions.ex_title: Mark (T) or (X) for each statement, with correction if needed
questions.q_txt: The density of petroleum oil is greater than the density of water.

questions.ex_title: Mark (T) or (X) for each statement, with correction if needed
questions.q_txt: Oxidation and reduction processes are two separate processes that do not occur together at the same time in the chemical reaction.

a_txt.mistake: two separate processes that do not occur together
a_txt.correction: two simultaneous processes that occur together

questions.ex_title: Mark (T) or (X) for each statement, with correction if needed
questions.q_txt: Redox reactions cause spoiling of food.
Ignition of glycerin using potassium permanganate .............
mcq_choices: occurs due to the activity of bacterial decomposition.
is accompanied by the formation of a purple-coloured flame.
requires the presence of an ignition source.

questions.q_txt: All the following are principles of extinguishing, except .............
Covering frying pan fires with a metal lid extinguishes the fire because .............
mcq_choices: the lid absorbs the heat of the flame.
the lid prevents air from reaching the fire.
the lid lowers the temperature of the fire.

questions.q_txt: The gas that neither burns nor supports combustion is .............
Extinguishing forest fires with water achieves one of the main principles of fire extinguishing, which is .............
The equation: Mg + CuO -> MgO + Cu represents ............. reaction.
From the reaction: 2Mg + CO2 -> 2MgO + C Which of the following is correct?
mcq_choices: Both magnesium and carbon dioxide are oxidized.
Carbon dioxide is oxidized and magnesium is reduced.
Magnesium is oxidized and carbon dioxide is reduced.
Both magnesium and carbon dioxide are reduced.

questions.q_txt: In the reaction: Mg + ZnO -> MgO + Zn Which substance undergoes oxidation?
What is the oxidizing agent in this reaction: PbO2 + 2H2 -> Pb + 2H2O?
The following equation: CH4 + 2O2 -> CO2 + 2H2O represents .............
mcq_choices: a combustion and a redox reactions.

questions.ex_title: What is meant by each of the following
questions.q_txt: Spontaneous combustion.
questions.a_txt: It is the ignition of materials without the presence of an external source of heat.

questions.ex_title: What is meant by each of the following
questions.q_txt: Oxidation process.
questions.a_txt: It is the process of the combination of fuel with oxygen which is usually accompanied by the emission of light, heat or both.

questions.ex_title: What is meant by each of the following
questions.q_txt: Reduction process.
questions.a_txt: It is the process of the combination of fuel with oxygen which is usually accompanied by the emission of light, heat or both.

questions.ex_title: What is meant by each of the following
questions.q_txt: Oxidizing agent.
questions.a_txt: It is the substance that gives (donates) oxygen to, or takes hydrogen away from another substance during the chemical reaction.

questions.ex_title: What is meant by each of the following
questions.q_txt: Reducing agent.
questions.a_txt: It is the substance that gives (donates) hydrogen to, or takes oxygen away from another substance during the chemical reaction.

questions.ex_title: Mention the importance (or use) of each of the following
questions.q_txt: Fire extinguishers.
questions.a_txt: Used to isolate fires from oxygen of atmospheric air.

questions.ex_title: Mention the importance (or use) of each of the following
questions.q_txt: Sand in chemical laboratories.
questions.a_txt: Used to extinguish fires of some metals such as sodium and magnesium.

questions.ex_title: Mention the importance (or use) of each of the following
questions.q_txt: Closing gas valves in case of fires.
questions.a_txt: Removal of fuel.

questions.q_txt: The car engine cannot operate without fuel.
questions.a_txt: Because it is a source of energy.

questions.q_txt: Frying pan fires are extinguished by covering them with a metal lid.
questions.a_txt: To isolate oxygen and thus the fire triangle is incomplete and the fire is extinguished.

questions.q_txt: The presence of a bucket filled with sand in school laboratories.
questions.a_txt: To extinguish fires of some metals such as sodium and magnesium by isolating them from oxygen.

questions.q_txt: It is strictly forbidden to extinguish sodium or potassium fires with water.
questions.a_txt: Because water increases the intensity of the fire instead of extinguishing it as a result of its reaction with these metals in an exothermic reaction accompanied by the ignition of the released hydrogen.

questions.q_txt: Carbon dioxide gas is used in extinguishing fires.
questions.a_txt: Because it is a gas that does not burn and does not support burning.

questions.q_txt: Water is used in extinguishing paper and wood fires.
questions.a_txt: Because of the high specific heat of water, which makes it absorbs a large amount of thermal energy and thus the temperature of the burning materials decreases.

questions.q_txt: Water is not used in extinguishing of petroleum oil fires.
questions.a_txt: Because petroleum oil floats on the surface of water due to its lower density compared to that of water, which leads to the spread of fires instead of extinguishing them.

questions.q_txt: Air evacuation preserves food for a longer period of time.
questions.a_txt: Because this provides a low-oxygen environment, which reduces oxidation and reduction reactions that lead to spoiling of food.

questions.q_txt: Oxidation and reduction processes are two simultaneous processes.
questions.a_txt: Because when a substance gains oxygen (is oxidized), another substance must lose this oxygen (is reduced), and when a substance loses hydrogen (is oxidized), another substance must gain this hydrogen (is reduced).

questions.q_txt: Drops of glycerin are added to potassium permanganate powder.
questions.a_txt: A spontaneous exothermic reaction occurs, leading to the emission of smoke with the formation of a purple-coloured flame.

questions.q_txt: Water is used in extinguishing of petroleum oil fires.
questions.a_txt: Petroleum oil floats on the surface of water, which leads to the spread of fires instead of extinguishing them.

questions.q_txt: A stream of air is passed over red-hot copper powder.
questions.a_txt: Copper combines with oxygen of atmospheric air forming black copper oxide compound.

questions.q_txt: A stream of hydrogen gas is passed over black copper oxide with heating.
questions.a_txt: Reddish-brown copper is formed.

questions.ex_title: Study the following figures, then answer
questions.q_txt: How do you explain: the continuation burning of the candle (X)?
questions.a_txt: Due to the abundance of oxygen, which is the main element in the combustion process.

questions.ex_title: Study the following figures, then answer
questions.q_txt: What is the principle of extinguishing fire that was achieved in the case of the candle (Y)?
questions.a_txt: The principle of oxygen isolation.

questions.ex_title: Study the following figures, then answer
questions.q_txt: What is the change that occurs to the colour of copper? Explain.
questions.a_txt: It turns from reddish-brown to black / Because copper combines with oxygen of atmospheric air, forming black copper oxide compound.

questions.ex_title: Study the following figures, then answer
questions.q_txt: Write the reaction equation, illustrating on which the oxidizing agent, the reducing agent, and the oxidation-reduction processes.
questions.a_txt: 2Cu + O2 -> 2CuO (Oxidation process, Reduction process, Oxidizing agent, Reducing agent)

questions.q_txt: Mention the main principles of extinguishing fire.
questions.a_txt: Fuel removal, Smothering, Cooling.

questions.q_txt: Mention an application for each principle, illustrating the element that is removed from the combustion triangle.
questions.a_txt: Fuel removal: Closing gas valves when fires occur. Smothering: Extinguishing frying pan fires by covering them with a metal lid. Cooling: Extinguishing forest fires with water.

questions.q_txt: Mention a technological application for the redox reactions.
questions.a_txt: Preserving food by air evacuation.

questions.q_txt: In the reaction: Hydrogen + copper oxide -> Water vapour + copper (1) What is the process which each of the following undergoes? 1- Black copper oxide. 2- Hydrogen gas.
questions.a_txt: 1- Reduction process. 2- Oxidation process.

questions.q_txt: (2) Write the symbolic chemical equation which expresses the previous reaction.
questions.a_txt: CuO + H2 -> Cu + H2O

questions.q_txt: (3) Why is copper oxide considered an oxidizing agent and hydrogen gas a reducing agent?
questions.a_txt: Because copper oxide gave oxygen to hydrogen, turning into copper, while at the same time hydrogen removed oxygen from copper oxide, turning into water vapour.

questions.q_txt: The opposite equation represents a redox reaction: 2H2S + SO2 -> 3S + 2H2O (1) Complete: 1- The process (X) represents _____ process. 2- The process (Y) represents _____ process.
(2) Identify the oxidizing agent and the reducing agent in this reaction.
questions.a_txt: Oxidizing agent: SO2, Reducing agent: H2S
